summary: Policy-backed control for Steps Recorder. Microsoft documents the same device policy path and value name that the app writes, and the policy description matches the disabled behavior.

setting.name: Steps Recorder
setting.casual_explanation: Steps Recorder can capture user actions and screenshots to help explain a problem. This policy decides whether the feature is available.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it on privacy-focused or locked-down systems because it can capture sensitive on-screen actions and data.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning it off removes an easy built-in troubleshooting recorder, but it also reduces the chance of user action recordings being created on the device.
setting.professional_notes: This setting maps to DisableUAR under Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\AppCompat. AppCompat.admx and the ADMX_AppCompat Policy CSP document that enabling the policy disables Steps Recorder.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Steps Recorder remains available because the policy is not forcing it off.

allowed_values.label: Steps Recorder allowed
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled and Steps Recorder stays enabled.

allowed_values.label: Steps Recorder disabled
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is enabled and Steps Recorder is turned off.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes DisableUAR = 1, which matches the documented disable behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows device-policy default

states.rationale: The device policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: Not configured corresponds to Steps Recorder staying available.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Users who want the built-in troubleshooting tool available
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Steps Recorder available for support and troubleshooting.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You do not want user action recordings to be created.
tradeoffs: No privacy lock-down is enforced for this feature.

recommended_profiles.label: No user action recording
avoid_for: Help desk scenarios that rely on Steps Recorder
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to prevent the feature from recording user actions and screenshots.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a built-in guided tool for capturing reproduction steps.
tradeoffs: Support workflows may need another screen capture or reporting tool.

evidence.title: Microsoft ADMX_AppCompat Policy CSP
evidence.summary: Official documentation for the policy that controls the Steps Recorder state.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft AppCompat.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file shows DisableUAR with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0 under the AppCompat policy key.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes DisableUAR = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

evidence.title: nohuto win-config mirror - DisableUAR policy
evidence.summary: DataCollection.admx shows DisableUAR under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\AppCompat. The mirrored ExplainText says enabling the policy disables Steps Recorder, while disabling or not configuring it leaves Steps Recorder enabled. EnabledValue=1 and DisabledValue=0 are explicit.

decision.why: Microsoft documents AppCompatTurnOffUserActionRecord directly in the ADMX_AppCompat Policy CSP and maps it to Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\AppCompat\DisableUAR. The same page describes that enabling the policy turns off Steps Recorder, and the app writes that exact machine policy surface.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Turn off Steps Recorder. This policy setting enables or disables Steps Recorder (Steps Recorder was called Problem Steps Recorder in Windows 7). If you enable this policy setting, Steps Recorder does not start when a user runs Psr.exe or clicks Start Record. ADMX mapping: Registry Key Name Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\AppCompat; Registry Value Name DisableUAR.
validation_proof.notes: The ADMX_AppCompat Policy CSP page explicitly names the exact machine policy path and value name the app writes, and its policy description matches the disabled behavior. Added nohuto mirror corroboration via nohuto-uar-admx.
